fechar.reason: cuidado wrongly implies Spanish 'fechar' isn't used for 'poner fecha' (dating a document) and that only 'datar' works. In fact Spanish 'fechar' is a standard verb meaning 'to date/put a date on' (e.g. 'fechar una carta'), so the parenthetical '(eso es datar)' misstates Spanish vocabulary even though the core PT/ES semantic gap (cerrar vs fechar-fecha) is real.

paquete.reason: Unable to verify 'paquete' as genuine Brazilian slang for menstruation; known BR-PT euphemisms are 'chico'/'tio Chico chegou', 'regra', 'está naqueles dias', etc. Not 'paquete'. The item, gloss, and both example sentences all hinge on this unverified/likely-fabricated slang sense, and the vulgar/rare hedge in notes reads as cover for an invented entry — defaulting to fail per uncertainty rule since a wrong vocabulary item here is high-risk.
